question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that causes AbstractStreamFileParser.parse() to catch an exception in the catch block, set success=false, and re-throw, but the finally block's timer.record() call itself throws an exception (e.g., due to a corrupted MeterRegistry state), masking the original error and leaving the parser in an undefined state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the ParserContext to hold references to extremely large byte arrays (e.g., large contract bytecode or file data), and when SqlEntityListener.onEnd() triggers flush(), the combined memory pressure causes a GC pause long enough to trigger the @Transactional timeout, rolling back the transaction and halting processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that AbstractStreamFileParser.parse(List<T>) iterates all files before calling doFlush() once, such that a crafted stream file early in the list causes doParse() to accumulate corrupt state in ParserContext, and when onEnd() is finally called, the flush() operation writes corrupt data to the database and throws, halting the node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause RedisPublisher.onEnd() to fail silently (log.error only) when publish() catches an Exception from redisOperations.executePipelined(), while simultaneously the topicMessagesQueue fills up because the consumer thread is stuck, eventually causing topicMessagesQueue.put() to block the main parse thread indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file that causes SyntheticLogListener.onEnd() to call updater.updateContractLog(entityMap) for a large number of SyntheticLogUpdater instances, each performing EVM address lookups and bloom filter computations, causing CPU exhaustion that starves other processing threads and contributes to node shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the generic type parameter T extends StreamFile<?> in StreamFileListener to cause a ClassCastException at runtime if a listener implementation receives a StreamFile subtype it does not expect, propagating through the CompositeRecordStreamFileListener and crashing the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause AbstractStreamFileParser.doFlush() to call streamFileListener.onEnd(streamFile) where streamFile.clear() has already been called (e.g., due to a race condition or double-processing), causing onEnd() to operate on a cleared/null state and throw a NullPointerException that halts the parser?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause SqlEntityListener.onNftTransferList() to call flushNftState() followed by nftRepository.updateTreasury(), and when onEnd() subsequently calls flush(), a constraint violation from the partial NFT state flush propagates as a ParserException and halts the processing node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that causes the parseLatencyMetric.record() call in AbstractStreamFileParser.parse() to receive a negative Duration (if consensusEnd is in the future), causing a metric recording exception that propagates and crashes the parse loop?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RecordFileParser.parse() calls parserContext.clear() in a finally block, but if onEnd() throws before the finally block of the outer try in AbstractStreamFileParser executes, the parserContext may not be cleared, causing stale data to accumulate and corrupt the next parse cycle?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file that causes SqlEntityListener.onEnd() to call flush() which calls context.forEach(batchPersister::persist), and if batchPersister.persist() is called with an empty collection, does it throw an unexpected exception that propagates through onEnd() and halts the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause AbstractStreamFileParser.parse(List<T>) to process a batch where shouldParse() returns false for all files (due to manipulated consensusEnd/consensusStart values), causing doFlush() to never be called and onEnd() to never execute, permanently stalling the listener state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Order annotations on listener implementations (RedisPublisher @Order(0), SyntheticLogListener @Order(2), SqlEntityListener @Order(3)) such that a failure in a lower-order listener prevents higher-order listeners from executing, leaving the database in an inconsistent state that causes future onEnd() calls to fail?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause RedisPublisher's single-threaded executor to die due to an unhandled exception in the publish() method (despite the try-catch, if an Error is thrown), causing the topicMessagesQueue consumer to stop, the queue to fill up, and topicMessagesQueue.put() to block the main parse thread indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause SqlEntityListener.mergeEntity() to be called with conflicting entity states, producing an entity object with an invalid timestamp range that causes a database constraint violation during flush() in onEnd(), propagating as a ParserException and halting the node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that causes AbstractStreamFileParser.parse() to log an error message containing user-controlled data (streamFile.getName()) via log.error(), and if the logging framework has a vulnerability (e.g., log4shell-style), exploit it to crash the importer process on multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file that causes SyntheticLogListener.onEnd() to call parserContext.getTransient(SyntheticLogUpdater.class) returning a very large list, and then for each updater call updateContractLog() which performs multiple cache lookups, causing a memory spike that triggers an OutOfMemoryError and crashes the JVM?
